chapter: Methods of Separation in Everyday Life

topics.topic: Methods of Separation in Everyday Life

cards.text: The method of picking by hand from a mixture (when two or more substances are mixed) such as small stones and husk from wheat and rice is called handpicking.
The method of separating heavier and lighter components of a mixture by wind or by blowing air is called winnowing.
The process of separating tea leaves from tea is called filtration.
Separation of magnetic and nonmagnetic substances by using a magnet is called magnetic separation.

Malli and Valli's Vacation Plans
Malli and his sister Valli are excited for their summer vacation and plan to travel across India visiting relatives and friends. They always ensure to stay in touch with their loved ones.

First Visit to Nani's Home
Their initial stop is at their Nani's house in Haryana, surrounded by large fields with a variety of grains piled up. Observing the process of handpicking stones and husk from grains, they learn about the importance of removing impurities from food items.

The Method of Handpicking
  • Handpicking is separating solid materials based on differences in size, color, and shape.
  • It is effective when the unwanted particles are present in small quantities and easily distinguishable by hand.

Field Visit and Threshing
At the fields, Malli and Valli observe farmers using threshing to separate grains from wheat stalks, a process where stalks are beaten to free the grains.

The Process of Winnowing
Winnowing involves the separation of lighter husk from heavier grains using wind or air, traditionally done with a bamboo tray.

Advanced Threshing Techniques
With technological development, threshers have been developed. These machines automate the process of threshing and winnowing.

Kneading Flour and Sieving
While preparing meethi puris, the use of sieving removes bran and small stones from flour. This is a process that separates fine particles from coarser ones.

Evaporation in Salt Extraction
To obtain common salt from seawater, evaporation helps by converting water into vapor, leaving behind solid residues, which are then purified.

Ayurveda and Medicinal Preparation
In Ayurveda, plants are dried using evaporation, enhancing the medicinal properties by removing excess moisture.

Decantation and Sedimentation in Tea Making
Allowing tea leaves to settle is sedimentation, and pouring the liquid without disturbing the sediment is called decantation.

Filtration in Liquid Purification
Filtration separates insoluble solids from liquids, allowing clear liquid to pass through, leaving solids behind on a filter.

Churning Curd to Butter
While observing a traditional churning process, butter is extracted from curd by rotating it to separate the lighter butterfat.

Magnetic Separation Techniques
  • Magnetic separation uses magnets to separate magnetic materials from non-magnetic ones.
  • This technique is applied in recycling industries to separate scrap iron from waste.

Recycling and Environmental Awareness
Familiarization with environmental impacts from seeing plastic pollution urges the importance of proper waste management.

Memories from Bharat ki Yatra
The holiday's experiences not only include family visits but also lessons on diverse methods of separating substances, enriching their knowledge and creating lasting memories.
The process in which the stalks are beaten to separate grains from them is called threshing.
The method of separating lighter husk from heavier grains by wind or blowing air is called winnowing.
The process of separating solids from a mixture based on variations in particle size using a sieve is called sieving.
The process of settling down of heavier insoluble component at the bottom of a liquid is called sedimentation. When the liquid is removed by tilting the vessel, the process is called decantation.
Filtration can be used to separate insoluble solid components from a liquid.
Churning is used to extract butter from curd.
